body:
## Purpose 
Cleans up how we run the soil in integrated models
Adds a snow soil integrated model and runs it at a site

A next step could be (1) to try to see how this "land_components" field could be added to the Canopy and snow models (from a refactoring point of view) or (2) Add in the snow/soil/canopy model using the same framework. We may find that (2) requires (1), but that attempting (2) first makes it clearer how to do (1). We can also rename AtmosDrivenFluxBC then if it makes sense and we want to.

A future PR can also add in the heat flux between snow and soil (right now snow affects soil via a meltwater flux and via the snow cover fraction only, soil does not affect snow because the ground heat flux is 0 and water does not move from soil to snow)

## To-do
Review
Run long runs to check behavior

## Content
1. Adds an empirical rain/snow split to fluxnet data
2. Adds a script running the snow/soil model at the Ozark site, and updates buildkite to include this. Checks conservation of energy and mass
3. Adds `prognostic_land_components` as a field to the AtmosDrivenFluxBC struct. We then dispatch off of the *value* of this when computing the BC due to AtmosDrivenFluxBC, since these computations change for values of (:soil,), or (:snow, :soil), or for (:canopy, :soil, :soilco2).
4. Adds in a "sublimation_source" helper function which adds in SoilSublimation for any AtmosDrivenFluxBC without snow, adds in SoilSublimationWithSnow for the snow case.
5. Creates the snow/soil "LandHydrology" model.

## Alternative

Here is an option where I had to introduce AtmosDrivenFluxBCWithSnow
https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaLand.jl/blob/fac9004afa5676266b34b7e30e8c3ab760072b59/src/integrated/soil_snow_model.jl
Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

----
- [ ] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: Integrated snow and soil modeling - Option 2
